_meta.owner_note: Every term carries a one-line rationale (category -- why this term); these lines are the raw material for methodology.md section 2. Post-freeze changes go in the methodology changelog with a date and a reason.
_meta.ex_ante_rule: No retrospective event names (Galwan, Pulwama, Balakot, Sindoor, Kargil, Uri, 26/11, Doklam...). Enforced by tests/test_dictionaries.py. Spikes at those events must be DETECTED by structural terms, not baked in.
_meta.query_grammar: GDELT DOC API: OR only inside parens, no nesting, no AND/OR mixing inside a group. Therefore every term is a single quoted phrase; per-channel disambiguation uses one 'anchor' word ANDed outside the OR group. Hyphenated text tokenizes to spaces, so "India Pakistan border" also matches 'India-Pakistan border'.

rationale.\"Line of Control\": geography -- the standing name of the militarised Kashmir boundary; near-universal in escalation coverage
rationale.\"India Pakistan border\": geography -- the generic international-press phrasing (also matches 'India-Pakistan border')
rationale.\"Pakistan occupied Kashmir\": geography -- Indian-press variant for the disputed territory
rationale.\"Pakistan administered Kashmir\": geography -- international-press variant for the same territory; both needed for recall
rationale.\"ceasefire violations\": doctrine -- the recurring LoC vocabulary; anchor 'India' excludes Gaza/Ukraine ceasefire coverage
rationale.\"ceasefire violation\": doctrine -- singular form; GDELT phrases do not stem, so both forms are needed
rationale.\"cross-border terrorism\": doctrine -- India's standing diplomatic framing, reused across two decades of coverage
rationale.\"cross-border firing\": doctrine -- the wire-service phrase for LoC exchanges
rationale.\"cross-border shelling\": doctrine -- artillery variant of the same press vocabulary
rationale.\"infiltration bid\": doctrine -- the recurring Indian-press phrase for LoC crossing attempts
rationale.\"surgical strikes\": doctrine -- response vocabulary reused across escalations; a phrase, not an event name (borderline case, discussed in methodology s2)
rationale.\"Inter-Services Intelligence\": institution -- the actor named in terrorism-linkage coverage; anchor keeps it India-related, not Afghanistan-related
rationale.\"Indus Waters Treaty\": institution -- the standing water-sharing mechanism whose stress signals escalation
rationale.\"Siachen\": geography -- the standing high-altitude military deployment

rationale.\"Line of Actual Control\": geography -- the standing name of the disputed boundary; the channel's core term
rationale.\"India China border\": geography -- generic international phrasing (also matches 'India-China border')
rationale.\"eastern Ladakh\": geography -- the western-sector name where standoff coverage concentrates; a place, not an event name
rationale.\"Arunachal Pradesh\": geography -- the eastern disputed sector; with the India anchor, global coverage is dominated by the dispute, not state politics
rationale.\"Tawang\": geography -- the standing focal town of the eastern-sector dispute
rationale.\"People's Liberation Army\": institution -- the force; anchor 'India' excludes Taiwan-only PLA coverage
rationale.\"border standoff\": doctrine -- the recurring press phrase for LAC confrontations; anchored to India
rationale.\"India China relations\": institution -- catches the diplomatic/talks news cycle that geography terms miss
rationale.\"disengagement\": doctrine -- the specific vocabulary of LAC de-escalation coverage; anchored to India

rationale.\"Strait of Hormuz\": chokepoint -- the single most important term: the passage for the bulk of India's crude imports
rationale.\"Gulf of Oman\": chokepoint -- the adjacent waterway where tanker incidents recur
rationale.\"Gulf tensions\": doctrine -- the recurring headline phrase for regional escalation
rationale.\"Middle East conflict\": doctrine -- the generic press frame; high recall for the channel's topic
rationale.\"Israel Iran\": doctrine -- matches 'Israel-Iran conflict/tensions/war' via hyphen tokenization
rationale.\"Iran Israel\": doctrine -- the reverse word order; both orders appear in copy
rationale.\"Iran sanctions\": institution -- the standing sanctions regime governing an Indian supply option
rationale.\"energy security\": doctrine -- the policy vocabulary; broader than the Gulf, accepted and disclosed in methodology s2
rationale.\"oil supply disruption\": doctrine -- the market-press phrase for the risk this channel tracks
rationale.\"oil supply disruptions\": doctrine -- plural form; no stemming in GDELT phrases
rationale.\"crude oil supply\": doctrine -- supply-side coverage, avoiding the price-only news the channel excludes

rationale.\"US tariffs\": doctrine -- the robust bigram in tariff coverage; anchor 'India' scopes it to India-relevant stories
rationale.\"reciprocal tariffs\": doctrine -- standing trade-policy vocabulary that long predates any single administration
rationale.\"trade deal\": institution -- catches the negotiation news cycle; India-anchored
rationale.\"trade war\": doctrine -- escalation vocabulary; India-anchored so only India-relevant coverage counts
rationale.\"Section 301\": institution -- the standing US trade-law instrument, used against India before
rationale.\"CAATSA\": institution -- the US sanctions statute hanging over India's Russian defence purchases since 2017
rationale.\"H-1B visa\": institution -- the standing services-trade friction point
rationale.\"secondary sanctions\": doctrine -- the vocabulary of extraterritorial pressure on India's Russia trade
rationale.\"Russian oil\": doctrine -- India-anchored; assigned here rather than gulf_energy because the risk vector is US policy, not physical supply (bleed decision, methodology s2)

rationale.\"Red Sea shipping\": chokepoint -- the standing compound in rerouting/crisis coverage; bare 'Red Sea' would import tourism and environment coverage
rationale.\"Red Sea attacks\": doctrine -- the attack-coverage variant of the same story
rationale.\"Suez Canal\": chokepoint -- the canal itself; routine-transit coverage is modest and disclosed
rationale.\"Bab el-Mandeb\": chokepoint -- the Red Sea's southern gate ('Bab el Mandeb' also matches via tokenization)
rationale.\"Strait of Malacca\": chokepoint -- the eastern passage for India's Asia trade
rationale.\"Cape of Good Hope\": geography -- the rerouting marker: diversion coverage names it constantly
rationale.\"Houthi attacks\": actor -- the group whose signature is shipping attack; an actor name, not an event name
rationale.\"shipping lanes\": doctrine -- the generic sea-lane security vocabulary
rationale.\"Somali pirates\": actor -- the recurring piracy story on India's western sea lanes
rationale.\"maritime security\": doctrine -- the policy vocabulary for the whole channel; broad, accepted and disclosed
